title: LatticeWave Support
preview: Inbound SMB lead. Self-serve signups already created trial org (3 developers). Primary use: chat-based customer support + internal doc search and answer summarization. POC ask: 4-week trial, ~5k messages/month, expected concurrency ~15-25 QPS during peak. Latency target: 95th percentile < 300ms for short chat responses (prompt + 256 tokens). Cost sensitivity: high — finance asked for per-conversation pricing estimate and recommendations for batching/caching. Security: legal asked for SOC2 Type II, full sub-processors list, and a DPA template to review. Also asked about data residency (prefer US only), audit logs retention (90 days), and whether Redwood uses any third-party NLP enrichment vendors. Quote from Head of Eng: "We can move fast if SOC2 and DPA look clean — small ARR but rapid scale potential if cost/perf works." AE notes: prospect is open to hosted API only (no VPC/private) for now. Next deliverables: SOC2 summary doc + sub-processor spreadsheet + DPA template (AE to route to legal). SE to prepare short runbook for optimizing token costs (batching + prefix caching suggestions). Watchlist: competitor trials with OpenAI; prospect evaluating latency & cost tradeoffs. CRM style fragments: "needs SOC2 before procurement", "will sign DPA if subproc list clears", "trial org active: 3 seats", "region: GCP us-east1 preferred".

title: BryteBridge Marketing Platform
preview:
Discovery notes (concise):
- Inbound from content marketing campaign (signup form + demo request).
- Company: BryteBridge — SMB marketing platform that auto-generates campaign content (multi-tenant SaaS for small brands).
- Primary ask: high-volume, low-cost text generation for daily newsletter + social + landing page variants.
- Volume: estimated 8M tokens/month in steady state; initial POC target = 1.2M tokens/month.
- Latency/throughput: p50 target <250ms for single prompt flows used by editor UI; bulk batch jobs run asynchronously (throughput 200-400 reqs/sec during batch windows).
- Cost sensitivity: high. Current vendor spend ~ $2.5k/month; want better unit economics at scale.
- Technical preference: open-models/quantized variants acceptable; interested in Redwood's model catalog + quantization guidance.
- Critical features called out: prefix/KV caching for template-heavy content, automatic batching for burst windows, deterministic routing to minimize cost, and simple REST/SDK integration for self-serve.
- Security: SOC2 acceptable, want audit logs and basic retention controls. No air-gapped needs; public cloud only.
- Quote from call: "We need predictable per-item pricing — otherwise ops margin vanishes at scale."
- Product fit: Hosted API looks aligned with self-serve motion. Willing to start with credits/POC then convert if unit economics look right.
- POC plan: 2-week gated POC — deliver sample run using 100k tokens across representative templates, measure latency, token cost, and cache hit-rate.
- Integration notes: prefer node.js SDK; will plug into existing content pipeline that uses template placeholders and per-customer tuning.
- Observability ask: per-route token/cost breakdown, simple tracing for slow requests.
- Internal stakeholders: CTO (product owner), Growth lead (campaign owner), 1 backend eng for POC.
- Sales notes: AE (Ava Chen) intro call 2026-02-25; demo scheduled 2026-03-02 (recording + Fireflies). SE Liam joined for deep tech questions on 2026-02-27.
- Competitive posture: evaluating OpenAI and Cohere pricing + caching approaches.
- Risk: may stick with incumbent if Redwood POC doesn't show >=20% cost reduction or similar latency.
- Next steps (short): send tailored pricing sketch + example POC plan (by 2026-03-15), run 2-week POC, validate cache-hit improvements.

title: GnomeForge Systems
preview:
Inbound PLG lead from indie dev + micro-startup. Founder/CTO (Elliot Park) building a tiny multi-tenant coding assistant for indie SaaS customers. Signed up via docs and quickstart; no AE outreach yet. Key points: prefers hosted API (fast time-to-market), will likely stick with public cloud. Current sandbox usage: experimenting with chat + embeddings for code search. Quote from call: "We need something that 'just works' — latency under ~250ms p50 for short chats, and simple token billing we can pass to customers." Budget: very cost-sensitive; target < $1k/mo initially, with willingness to ramp if product-market fit. Wants to test smaller open models first (Llama-family, distilled) but may use Redwood-verified models if cost/perf is right. Security: expects basic audit logs and 30d retention; not asking for VPC or private deployment now. Operational concerns raised: rate limits per sub-tenant, ability to rotate keys programmatically, and one-off high-burst days (deploy-time traffic). POC asks: sample prompts for code assistant, recommended model/quantization profile for low-cost inference, batching suggestions (automated vs manual).

CRM-style fragments/shorts observed in notes:
- "no AE yet; founder doing evals"
- "wants hosted only for now"
- "prefers US region/residency"
- "asks about token overflow protection"

Next small tactical actions (internal):
- Add to automated onboarding drip focusing on cost-preserving patterns (batching, cache), include short how-to for embedding-based code search.
- CS/growth to monitor usage; if >2k tokens/day create inbound AE alert.
- Provide quickstart snippet for rotating keys + sample prompt set for code assistant (attach to Drive doc).

title: EmberFox Payments
preview:
Inbound from product-led signup. Sees immediate value in embeddings for semantic search and chat assistant for merchant support.

Primary contact: CTO - Maya Patel (also handles infra/security). Quote: 'We need SOC2 evidence before procurement but can trial hosted API for 30 days.'

Latency/throughput: target p95 < 300ms for short chat turn, expect ~40-60 concurrent agents during business hours.

Model preference: cost-conscious; prefer quantized open models or small LLMs for initial deployment. Will test 2-3 variants.

Routing/fallback: interested in automatic fallback to cheaper model when load spikes; want easy pinning per API key.

Data handling: transactional data may include last4 PAN and customer notes => ask for redaction guidance and retention controls.

Security asks: SSO (SAML) integration, SOC2 report (type II) timeline, audit logs, KMS integration for key control. Data residency: US only for now.

POC plan: 2-week hosted POC using sample merchant transcripts + synthetic transaction data. Success metrics: intent accuracy > 85% for support intents, embedding recall@10 > 0.8 for merchant docs.

Billing: budget signalled $10k-$50k ARR if POC successful. Wants breakout of per-token cost vs embedding indexing cost.

Competitive notes: trialling OpenAI for baseline; Redwood needs to show lower cost for bulk embeddings and transparent performance numbers.

Action items: send SSO integration guide, share SOC2 redacted artefact (or SOC2 scope timeline), provide VPC cost estimation, provision POC API key and sample prompt templates.

title: BerryTrail AI
preview:
Lead source: inbound (community meetup & blog).
Summary: small ecommerce platform (~120 employees) building conversational product discovery + support assistant. Goal is to replace brittle keyword search with embeddings + reranker, then add a chat layer for product guidance. Very cost conscious but prioritizes non-hallucinating answers for SKU/spec info.
What we heard on 3/9 call:
- "Customer quote: 'We need reliable answers about SKUs and specs — hallucinations cost us returns.'"
- Current infra: frontend + search on AWS; wants hosted_api (public cloud) to prototype quickly, then evaluate Dedicated later if traffic grows.
- POC scope: embeddings for 1M product text tokens (catalog + specs + reviews), reranker for top-10, chat for post-search follow-up. Expected POC volume: ~50k queries/month.
- Latency targets: embeddings batch job OK to be minutes; chat interactions need p95 <= 250ms ideally; realistic goal 150-250ms for 7B variants. Throughput: spikes to ~30 req/s during promos.
- Model selection concerns: team asked about Llama-2-13B vs Mistral-7B-instruct vs Qwen-7B-chat. Questions: expected quality delta on product QA tasks, hallucination risk, token cost differences, compatibility with streaming and function calling, and whether Redwood can auto-fallback to cheaper variant when under load.
- Security: SSO (SAML) later in roadmap; SOC2 not mandatory but want audit logging and retention controls. No strict data residency requirement today.
- Integrations: wants embeddings in their vector DB (Pinecone/Weaviate) — asked about best practices for prefix caching and deduping.
POC milestones (tentative):
- Week 1: API keys + sample prompts + example embeddings backfill (self-serve using credits).
- Week 2-3: run baseline with Mistral/Qwen 7B for embeddings + rerank; gather quality metrics (MRR, precision@5).
- Week 4: test chat on 7B; if substantial quality gap, trial Llama-2-13B hosted variant for chat and compare latency/cost.
Open items / blockers:
- Need concrete hosted latency numbers for each model variant from Redwood benchmarks (requests for 7B vs 13B on public-hosted GPU pool).
- Finance needs rough monthly cost estimate to sign-off (range + breakeven vs Dedicated).
- Want a short guidance doc from Redwood: "When to pick 7B vs 13B for ecommerce product QAs".
Notes for AE/SE follow up: attach quick benchmark table, recommend initial config: hosted_api, model=mistral-instruct-7b, batching=auto, enable prefix caching for catalog, stream=false for initial tests. Schedule SE call wk of 3/15 to walk through POC checklist.
CRM quick tags: inbound, hosted-api, model-selection, embeddings, cost-sensitive

title: Call Cartography LLC
preview:
Prospect inbound from self-serve trial (signed up via quickstart). Early discovery call 2026-03-02. Primary product interest: hosted API for call-summary + downstream lead scoring. Key ask: PII handling and retention. Wants option to redact/scrub PII at ingest and configurable retention windows (30/90/365 days).

Call snippets (rep paraphrase): "We need to be sure transcripts with customer emails/cc numbers do not leak into embeddings or long-term storage." Said they will test with 1000 weekly calls. Cost sensitivity moderate — SMB budget. Latency target: median generation <800ms for 1.2k token conversations; willing to accept up to 1.5s for batched summaries.

Security: requested SOC2 report, KMS support, audit logs; asks about retention controls exposed in Console (UI + API). Legal sent initial questionnaire (attached on Drive).

Notes on integration: prototyping with webhooks + Redwood hosted API keys; wants single-tenant data isolation feel but will start public cloud hosted (no VPC) if retention rules and redaction available.

AE/SE action items: sent short checklist for PII redaction patterns; scheduled follow-up to review demo of retention UI (2026-03-15). Provided pricing deck and example token cost calc.

Tone: pragmatic, engineering-led buyer, product manager (Krista, Dir. of Product) is technical and cares about edge cases (call transcripts with SSNs).

Follow-up cadence: weekly until token spikes reduced or they commit to paid tier.

title: LatchPoint Labs
preview:
Lead source: inbound via blog post about low-latency chat widgets. Founder (Maya Rao) did initial sign-up and created API key but hasn't started high-volume calls. Small engineering team (2 engs) building an AI-first in-product assistant for their B2B dashboard.

Founder call summary (2026-03-05):
- Maya (founder/CTO) on call with AE + SE; very price sensitive — wants to understand real per-1M-token cost including caching/batching benefits.
- "We compare prices with OpenAI/Anthropic — but we also care about predictable latency and regional availability."
- Use-case: interactive chat + code snippet generation in app; average prompt length ~150 tokens, responses ~250 tokens. QPS expected low (0.5-2 rps) but spiky when customers onboard.
- Prefers hosted API for now (no infra ops), may consider Dedicated when ARR grows.
- Wants a simple calc: monthly dev/dev+prod token burn examples and cost at 100k, 500k, 1M tokens. Ask for guidance on model choice (smaller cheaper vs higher quality), fallback behavior during quota/latency spikes.

Security/Compliance: not regulated, but asked about SSO support for Console and audit logs. Will expect SOC2 roadmap info; asked about encryption/KMS for future.

Current sentiment: warm, founder-led, pragmatic. Main hesitation = unclear unit economics vs familiar vendors.

Quick wins agreed on call:
- Send tailored pricing examples + simple cost calculator sheet
- Share quickstart SDK snippet and Postman collection so dev can do a 30-min POC
- Add short note on batching/caching tips for low-latency interactive flows

Notes from AE (Ava): trial promo credits applied, invited to community Slack, scheduled optional follow-up demo week of 2026-03-15.

title: Cobalt Nimbus LLC
preview:
Inbound SMB lead. Self-serve signup on hosted API. Primary contact: Maria Lopez (CTO). Use case: conversational agent that calls internal tools (ticket search, run diagnostics) via function/tool-calls; need structured JSON output to map to internal RPC.

Summary of problem: In streaming + JSON_MODE the model sometimes emits function_call events in multiple chunks. The Node SDK handler receives first chunk containing 'function_call' and partial 'arguments' string; later chunks complete it. Customer's parser tries to JSON.parse on first visible chunk and fails with 'Unexpected token' errors. Also observed cases where model returns single quotes instead of double quotes inside arguments ("TypeError: Unexpected token '\' in JSON at position 0"). They attempted a server-side workaround to buffer until end_of_stream but that increases latency and complexity.

Customer also reports that when they enable strict function schema in their prompt, the model sometimes returns plain text instead of a function_call envelope, making schema validation fail with 'extraProperties' when they attempt to coerce into typed args.

What we've tried / suggested:
- Confirm Node SDK version (customer on 0.12.3) and suggested upgrade to latest; share streaming parser example.
- Reproducer repo requested (received 2026-03-10) with minimal server and client.
- Suggested approach: buffer per-request until 'DONE' event in stream or use stream event types to stitch function_call args before JSON.parse; SE will attempt to harden SDK example to make this easier.
- Discussed short-term workaround: disable JSON_MODE for function args and return as base64-encoded payload, then decode after full stream assembled (hacky but avoids parse errors).

Quotes / paraphrases: "We can accept a little overhead but need tool calls to start within 200ms of user action — can't wait for full buffer every time." — Maria L.

Open items: need raw chunk-level logs (we have partial), need exact SDK config and headers used, confirm if customer is using custom middleware that manipulates quotes. SE asked for HTTP-level capture (curl or tcpdump) to verify chunk boundaries.

Next steps: pairing session on 2026-03-14 to reproduce, capture raw stream, try patched SDK that defers JSON.parse until complete args stitched. If confirmed, provide code sample and suggest SDK patch/bug fix; if not solvable in hosted API SDK, propose short-term mitigation guidance for app-level buffering.

title: LittleBay Helpers
preview:
SMB inbound. Small product team (Customer Success automation startup). Primary asks: low-latency chat for support agents, embeddings for knowledge base search, and cheap reranking for triage. Wants public cloud hosted API (self-serve) — will consider Dedicated later if volume grows.

Model selection questions are the most active thread: they repeatedly asked for guidance on Llama vs Mistral vs Qwen and tradeoffs between 7B/13B/14B families. Key quotes: "Can we get chat-like quality at ~1/3 the cost?" and "We need p50 latency <300ms for short responses, p95 <800ms when longer."

Current thinking from customer: try Mistral-Instruct-7B for low cost, Llama-2-13B for highest helpfulness on instruction prompts, Qwen-14 if bilingual support needed (EN/CN). Concerned about token cost and tail latency on larger models. Asking about quantized variants and whether Redwood's hosted kernels give better perf than vanilla cloud endpoints.

Workload profile: peak 5-10 qps, typical prompt context 120 tokens, expected response 40-120 tokens. Embeddings pipeline separate - they used embeddings heavily in prototype and will want a single-vendor billing if possible.

Security: SOC2 required, SSO via SAML, audit logging retention for 6 months. Data residency not strict, prefer us-east region.

Action items from demo: SE to provide a model matrix (quality vs cost vs latency) tuned to their prompts, include sample token pricing at expected qps, propose a 48h smoke test on hosted API with 3 model variants (Mistral-7B quantized, Llama-13B standard, Qwen-14 opt). AE to follow up re: procurement timeline and credit top-up.

Stance: high product interest, high cost sensitivity. Likely to convert to low-tier paid hosted plan if Mistral/quantized option meets QA and latency.

title: ElmSpire KnowledgeWorks
preview:
Inbound via doc-search blog post; signed up for free tier and created first embedding index. Quick notes from discovery call: 
- CTO (Maya Singh) is primary technical contact; wants hosted API to avoid ops overhead.
- Primary workload: legal document search + contract Q&A (RAG). Low QPS (10-30 reqs/min) but need fast reranking for top-k (p95 latency target 150-250ms).
- Currently evaluating vector DBs: Pinecone + Weaviate; asking about recommended approach for reranking pipeline (embeddings -> ANN -> rerank via Hosted API LM).
- Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, expects usage to start small; asked about token pricing + embedding unit definitions.
- Model preference: open models preferred (Llama2 style), but open to Redwood-curated models if latency/cost are better.
- Security: requires SSO for dashboard, audit logs for queries, and ability to set retention to 90 days for PII. Not on-prem; public cloud only.
- Quote: "We want a simple onboarding path — if embeddings + reranking work we scale to dedicated later."
- Asked for example code integrating Pinecone + Redwood hosted embeddings + reranking call.
- Mentioned industry-specific concerns: redaction pipeline for contracts, opt-out for sensitive fields.
- No procurement yet, decision maker is CEO + Head of Engineering.
- Good fit for self-serve; likely to convert if PoC shows latency and costs align.

title: LichenLoop Technologies
preview:
Founder-led inbound (Maya Lin, CEO/CTO) — early discovery. Interested in Redwood hosted API for chat + embeddings; lean, cost-sensitive startup. Key points from 2026-03-03 call (Fireflies ff_20260303_lichenloop_discussion):
- Company background: launched beta of dev docs search and onboarding assistant in Jan. Team 7 engineers, Node.js backend. Expecting 5k monthly active users first two quarters.
- Primary workload: chat support widget (low concurrency, frequent short-turn messages) + backend embedding index for product docs (batch upload + incremental updates).
- Targets: average response latency <250ms for chat on p95; embedding indexing throughput not critical (>100 reqs/hr ok). Cost sensitive — founder quoted: 'If tokens cost us 3x OpenAI for embeddings we'll struggle.'
- Wants straightforward per-token pricing, clear quotas/rate limits, and predictable monthly bill (prefers usage caps + alerts). Concerned about hidden costs (e.g., KV cache misses, streaming overhead).
- Comparing options: currently testing OpenAI for chat + Cohere for embeddings; looked at Anthropic for safety. Wants a side-by-side cost/latency comparison for typical flows (chat session 200 tokens in/400 out; embed batch 50k tokens/month).
- Technical asks: Node.js SDK snippet, example curl, sample request/response size estimates, explanation of caching and token counting. Also wants guidance on batching embeddings to minimize cost.
- Security: requires SSO for admin console, audit logs, and simple data retention controls. No air-gapped/on-prem needs right now.
- Commercial: founder prefers self-serve sign-up and quick provisioning. Would consider upgrading to Dedicated once ARR justifies. Budget expectation: $1-10k ARR first year.
Recent timeline (shorthand):
- 2026-02-18: inbound signup, used free credits, basic tests with playground.
- 2026-02-25: AMA email from owner re: token pricing; asked about embedding discounts. (Gmail thread-1739af92)
- 2026-03-03: 30m discovery call (Fireflies ff_20260303_lichenloop_discussion). Founder on call, demoed current prototype.
- 2026-03-05: sent follow-up with basic pricing summary (internal draft).
- 2026-03-10: founder requested detailed comparison to OpenAI/Anthropic and Node.js quickstart.
Open items / blockers: pricing transparency vs OpenAI/Anthropic (need apples-to-apples calc), clarification on rate limits and concurrency for hosted tier, sample monthly cost estimate for their projected usage.
Next steps (short):
- Deliver hosted API pricing matrix + token calculator (Ava) — include typical chat/session and embeddings batching examples.
- Attach Node.js quickstart and example SDK code (SE: Diego).
- Offer 30m walk-through/demo wk of Mar 15; prepare a short perf/cost slide comparing Redwood hosted vs OpenAI/Anthropic for their workloads (use their sample numbers).
Stated quotes/snippets: 'We like the idea of unified API — but show me how much it'll cost compared to OpenAI.' — Maya L.
CRM tags/notes: founder-led, self-serve, pricing-sensitive, public-cloud-only, embeddings-heavy.

title: Ironbridge Medical AI
preview:
CRM notes:
- Company: Ironbridge Medical AI (large regional health system running AI for clinician workflow).
- Primary pain: strict regulatory + residency requirements across US & EU, must be able to run inference in customer VPC.
- Key ask: Private VPC deployment with SSO (SAML/Okta), SCIM provisioning for automated user/groups, and fine-grained RBAC exposed in Redwood Console/control plane mapped to AD groups.
- Security stack: customer uses AWS (GovCloud for some workloads) + Splunk for SIEM. They require: customer-managed KMS keys (CMK), audit log streaming to Splunk/CloudWatch, 7-year retention for logs, and SOC2/ISO27001 evidence.
- Quote from CISO: "No console admin user that isn't bound to our AD groups — full RBAC and SCIM mapping is non-negotiable."
- Technical notes from SE call: need SAML metadata template, SCIM provisioning test plan, sample mapping for role -> AD group -> Redwood role. title: BeaconFlow Labs
preview:
Account summary: BeaconFlow Labs is a mid-market B2B product company building an in-app onboarding copilot (guided flows + micro-tips). Evaluating Redwood Hosted API first to prove latency/cost, then committed interest in Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation.

Key constraints / asks: 
- Latency SLOs: p50 target ~120-200ms, p95 target <500ms for interactive onboarding steps; streaming + function-calls must be consistent (users notice variance).
- Cost controls: token cost caps per onboarding session (target <= $0.15/session initially), need per-route token caps and throttles; interested in Optimize recommendations (batching/caching) to reduce unit cost.
- Tooling: must support function/tool calling to internal product metadata + CRM (tokenized access), streaming partial responses to UI while tool calls resolve.
- Security: SSO/SAML, SOC2 posture important, KMS for key management, audit logs retention policy. Data residency: US preferred for now.

POC plan: 2-week hosted run of 10k sessions simulated from production-like traffic, measure p50/p95 token usage, streaming latency, function-call end-to-end RTT. If pass, move to Dedicated quote and capacity sizing.

Call notes (shorthand):
- Elena: "Users drop off when assistant pauses > 500ms" -> strong need for consistent tail latency.
- SE (Ravi): suggested prefix/KV caching for repeated onboarding flows; showed batching configs.
- Procurement: price sensitivity on per-token for high-volume onboarding; wants Dedicated uplift only if unit economics improve or SLAs locked.

Linked artefacts: pricing deck, hosted-vs-dedicated TCO sheet, security FAQ, Fireflies transcripts (see linked_fireflies).

Next immediate actions: send hosted POC credentials, provide sample streaming config and recommended token caps per flow, schedule weekly POC syncs.

title: Hearthline Postsale
preview:
Quick CRM notes, rough and chatty.
- Company: mid-market ecommerce platform focused on post-purchase experience (returns + shipping).
- Primary pain: high touch manual returns chats, CS costs spike during seasonal events (Nov-Dec). Want to automate ~60% of inbound returns flows w/ safe tool-calling to order service.
- P95 latency is a gating metric: they want reliable p95 for user-facing assistant. Target we discussed: first-token streaming <= 200ms, end-to-end p95 for short-turn responses (<=120 tokens) <= 600ms under normal load; tolerate higher during brief peaks if fallback works.
- Throughput: typical steady-state ~25-40 RPS, expected seasonal peaks up to 350-500 RPS per region (NA). Concurrency: ~120 concurrent live chats typical.
- Model preferences: prefer open/LLM variants they can run on Dedicated later (Llama-2-Chat style), but open to Redwood hosted for POC for speed. Want quantized fallback for burst.
- Tool calling: must call internal order microservice (read-only order status, shipping ETA) and escalate to human with context. Need idempotent calls + scrub PII.
- Security: SSO/SAML for staff, audit logs for all tool calls, KMS for any keys in private deployment. Data residency discussion for EU customers (they have EU merchants).
- Cost sensitivity: mid; willing to pay for predictable latency. Interested in Redwood Optimize suggestions for batching and quantization to lower costs.
- Quote from VP Product: "We need confident p95 numbers — if you can show stable p95 under our sample weekend load, the procurement cycle moves fast."
- Internal champion: Head of Support Ops (Emily Park) + Product manager (Ravi Singh). AE to loop in procurement after POC.
- Docs linked: POC prompt set, sample transcripts, NF logs to mimic (in Drive).

title: Emberfield KnowledgeFlux
preview:
Quick CRM notes / call snippets:
- Business: Emberfield builds a knowledge-layer SaaS used by ~300 mid-market customers for in-app help & support automation.
- Current stack: Elasticsearch + FAISS for embeddings, custom cross-encoder on CPU for rerank (high cost, unpredictable tail latency).
- Problem: want to reduce per-query cost and tighten p95 latency at scale (peak traffic: ~600 QPS, avg 200 QPS). Index size currently ~350k canonical docs (mixed product docs + transcripts) and growing ~30%/yr.
- Desired architecture: hosted vector search (ANN) + Redwood-hosted reranker (cross-encoder) with configurable fallback to lightweight scoring. Goal: p95 end-to-end rerank+retrieve <200ms, target median <120ms.
- Cost targets: business wants cost-per-query (embeddings + rerank) <= $0.005 on average; willing to trade small latency increase for 2x cost reduction vs current infra.
- Routing/fallback: need automatic fallback to smaller cross-encoder or L2 lightweight model when burst capacity saturated. Also prefer region-aware routing (US + EU).
- Security: SSO (SAML), audit logs retained 13 months, KMS for key management, explicit data residency (EUR for EU accounts) — SOC2 in procurement checklist.
- Quote from CTO Maya Singh: 'If you can show consistent p95 under 200ms at 600 QPS with predictable pricing, we move to Dedicated next year.'
- POC ask: Redwood to provide cost projection, baseline latency numbers for hosted_api with quantized cross-encoder +/- batching, and guidance on batch sizing/kv-cache for their traffic profile.
- Integration notes: will test with existing FAISS export + OpenSearch metadata. Wants SDK examples for streaming rerank results and token-level cost breakdown.
- Internal: AE (Ava) to push pricing deck + POC SOW. SE (Diego) to spin up baseline test in Redwood sandbox. CSM (Priya) flagged for onboarding timeline if POC successful.
- Risks: regulatory customers require EU data residency; procurement expects detailed incident response SLA; cross-encoder cost unknown for sustained qps.
- Next step (short): Schedule POC kickoff (2 business days to prepare dataset & creds).

title: Elm Harbor Assistants
preview:
Inbound SMB lead via self-serve (free credits). Small engineering team (2 backend, 1 frontend). Primary product: chat-based support assistant + doc search across help center. Key pain: perceived slowness on user-facing chat — several customer complaints this week.

Observed behavior (customer reports / internal testing):
- Users in US experiencing ~600-900ms extra RTT vs EU users; account originally created with default region eu-west-1.
- Frontend shows request timeouts at ~12s (client-side) when prompts include long context + history (~1.5-3k tokens).
- Team tried increasing client timeout but worried about cost and UX.

What they've asked/said: 
- "We signed up fast, but users are saying the bot feels slow — is this something we can fix without moving infrastructure?" 
- Very cost-sensitive; prefer hosted API but may upgrade if clear perf wins.

Redwood quick wins suggested on call: 
- Region: suggest creating API key in us-west-2 region for US traffic; ran quick curl test (we provided sample) showed ~250ms median improvement in staging.
- Timeouts: recommend bumping client timeout to 30s temporarily for troubleshooting and using streaming to cut perceived latency for users.
- Prompt length: advise truncation of chat history to recent 3 turns + keep system prompt minimal; precompute embeddings for doc search to avoid sending huge context each request.
- Request-level settings: limit max_tokens for completions, reduce temperature for deterministic replies where applicable; enable chunked responses for long answers.
- Instrumentation: add simple timing on frontend to capture server vs network latency (we shared snippet).

Customer constraints/risks: 
- No enterprise contract; will not accept private deployment. Public cloud only.
- Needs SSO and basic audit logs (SOC2 planned but not required today).
- Low tolerance for trial cost creep.

Sales/next actions: 
- Send perf checklist + region test guide (drive doc).
- Schedule 30m troubleshooting with Marco + Ava to run region switch and sample prompt edits.
- If region swap + prompt changes cut p95 latency by >200ms, expect move to evaluation.

Quote from PO: "If we can make it snappier for US users without blowing our credits, we’ll commit to a paid plan."

title: Emerald Bridge Integrations
preview:
Summary: midsize SaaS platform that resells and integrates disparate helpdesk tools for retail clients. Primary ask: robust tool-calling to create/update tickets, trigger refunds via internal REST APIs, and escalate to L3 via Slack/Jira. POC scope narrow: Zendesk create/update + refund endpoint + automated escalation flow.

Requirements & constraints:
- Tool-calls must be idempotent and show audit trail (who/what/when).
- Refund flow goes through internal refunds API that touches PCI-adjacent services — legal demands scoping and tokenization; cannot store raw PAN.
- Must provide per-request trace IDs and tie AI-decisions to logs for 7+ months (audit_logging).
- EU customers require residency; prefer control plane in public_cloud but data plane in VPC/EU region.
- Burst reliability: peak 5x normal traffic for 20-40 minutes (holiday sale spikes). Need graceful degradation (fallback to queue/human) and pre-warming capacity.
- Latency: agent-assist generation under 300-400ms typical; synchronous ticket-create should return within 800ms p99 under baseline.
- Cost sensitivity: willing to pay for dedicated capacity for predictable bursts, but wants guidance via Optimize on batching/quantization tradeoffs.

Quotes + paraphrase from calls:
- "We need zero surprises during Black Friday — if the AI tries to double-refund a customer, that's a showstopper." — Head of Ops
- "Auditability is non-negotiable; legal needs to be able to map a refund to an explanation." — Legal

POC success criteria:
- Zendesk create/update working with correct idempotency token and rollback path shown in logs.
- Refund API invoked with masked payload; payment team approves scope.
- Synthetic spike test: system maintains p95 latency target for agent-assist; no more than 2% of tool-calls fail with graceful fallback to human queue.
- Security team signs off on SOC2 + KMS integration and confirms no PII stored in LLM logs.

Open questions / Action items:
- Provide detailed PCI scoping doc + KMS keyflow diagram (requested by 2026-03-12). (drive link above)
- Confirm residency architecture options (private plane or EU VPC) and estimated delta in monthly cost.
- Pricing: need a firm quote for Dedicated pool with burst allowance and 99.9% uptime SLA.

Stitch notes: short, chatty CRM style; next call penciled for 2026-03-15 to review sec docs and start POC env provisioning.

title: Honeycomb Sageworks
preview:
Account background:
- Founded 2018, product analytics SaaS for B2B marketplaces. Heavy emphasis on conversational product support and embedded search in product UI.
- Mid-market expansion: onboarding 30 new customers in last 12 months.
POC summary (Hosted API trial):
- Trial window: 2026-01-12 -> 2026-02-28. Ran support-bot + search rerank workloads against 200k session dataset.
- Observed traffic: avg QPS (user-facing inference): 3.2 QPS; typical daytime baseline 5–7 QPS; peak bursts up to 28 QPS sustained for ~90s during product launch tests.
- Concurrency: observed 18 concurrent streams during peaks; 24 concurrent warm KV-cached sessions in internal stress tests.
- Token mix: median prompt tokens 380, median completion tokens 120 -> average total ~500 tokens per call. Token distribution: ~76% prompt-side tokens (document context + metadata), 24% generation tokens.
- Workload split during POC: 55% support-chat (chat-style generation), 30% semantic search embeddings (batch embed jobs at night), 15% reranking (short completions).
- Models tried on hosted API: rdx-small-compat (fast), rdx-medium-qual (preferred), community Llama-2-70B for internal eval. Quality tradeoffs: rdx-medium-qual hit latency targets with acceptable throughput; larger variants increased per-token cost without proportional accuracy gains for current prompts.
POC learnings relevant to Dedicated sizing: 
- Prefers low-latency tail: p95 target <= 220ms for chat responses <200 tokens. Need KV/prefix caching and continuous batching to avoid spikes.
- Nightly embedding batch jobs currently use ~10k concurrent embed items; could be migrated to dedicated batch nodes to reduce interference.
- Expected steady-state growth: 2x in 6 months; peak spikes could reach ~50 QPS for product launches.
- Cost sensitivity: moderate-high; procurement wants modeled cost at commit tiers (monthly reserved) vs burst-on-hosted comparison.
Security & compliance notes:
- Requires SSO (SAML), SOC2 attestation, audit logs retention 1 year, optional EU data residency for EU customers. Legal already requested a data residency addendum.
